vBRIEFInfo.author: overdeck/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1982: Revive convoy review as a configurable opt-in — global/project/per-issue (quick stays default)

plan.title: Revive convoy review as a configurable opt-in — global/project/per-issue (quick stays default)
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: Full convoy review (4 sub-reviewers + synthesis) is parked behind a hardcoded isExtendedReviewEnabled()=false (review-agent.ts:885). Quick self-review is the only live mode. There is no way to opt into heavier review for issues that warrant it without slowing every issue. The convoy machinery is intact and now resume-aware, so it can be revived behind a real switch.
narratives.Proposal: Add a review-mode setting (quick | full) resolvable at three scopes — global config.yaml, project .pan.yaml, and a per-issue record field — through ONE synchronous resolver. Wire isExtendedReviewEnabled(issueId?) to that resolver, un-park the convoy fan-out behind the switch, pass issueId to the dashboard reviewer-tree gate so lanes appear for full-mode issues, add a `pan review mode` CLI setter for the per-issue scope, verify the existing per-harness resume transport covers convoy re-review, and re-verify the six parked convoy bugs against the current lifecycle. Quick review stays the unchanged default.
narratives.NonGoals:
- No GitHub-label mechanism for per-issue mode (record field chosen instead).
- No per-reviewer or per-spawn harness/model pickers (removed; do not reintroduce).
- No dashboard UI control for SETTING per-issue mode (CLI setter is the operator affordance; the dashboard only reads the resolved mode to show/hide lanes).
- No change to quick review's behavior or prompt — it stays the unchanged default.
- No cache-sharing convoy optimization (that is #1862).
- No change to the verdict / review-status write path (PAN-1988 host-owned write + reactive handoffs preserved).
- No session rotation on re-review (resume-by-default; PAN-1980 refuses rotation).

autoDecisions.summary: Per-issue override is a reviewMode field on the per-issue record (.pan/records/<issue>.json), not a GitHub label.
autoDecisions.rationale: isExtendedReviewEnabled is synchronous with two synchronous callers; the record has a sync read/write door (single-source-of-truth) and is git-portable, while a label has no sync read path and would force the function async, rippling through callers. The issue body explicitly permits this alternative.

autoDecisions.summary: Effective mode resolves per-issue -> project -> global -> quick through ONE helper resolveReviewMode(issueId?).
autoDecisions.rationale: Matches the PRD decision rule and the single-source-of-truth tenet (one resolver, not scattered reads).

autoDecisions.summary: WI-7 cross-harness re-review transport is verify-and-test, not new transport code.
autoDecisions.rationale: resumeAgent (agents.ts:4951+) already routes pi->FIFO, codex->retry, claude-code->supervisor/tmux; both quick-review resume and convoy sub-reviewer resume call resumeAgent and inherit it. Confirmed in code at planning time.

autoDecisions.summary: Operator affordance for the per-issue scope is a new `pan review mode <id> <quick|full>` CLI subcommand plus a /pan-review SKILL.md update.
autoDecisions.rationale: The per-issue record is pipeline-managed; hand-editing is awkward and 'selectable' requires an operator path. Global/project scopes use existing config editing.

autoDecisions.summary: The mode flag gates convoy on/off only; sub-reviewer models keep DEFAULT_ROLES.review.sub defaults.
autoDecisions.rationale: Provider-default-only harness; do not reintroduce per-reviewer pickers.

items.title: Add roles.review.mode (quick|full) config field with global+project resolution

narrative.Action: In src/lib/config-yaml.ts add `mode?: 'quick' | 'full'` to the RoleConfig interface (:403), set `mode: 'quick'` on DEFAULT_ROLES.review (:452-461), and extend validateRoleFields (:1709) to reject any roles.<role>.mode that is present and not 'quick' or 'full'. No merge-logic change: the existing mergeRoleConfig spread {...existing, ...roleConfig} (:1683-1705) already merges the field, and mergeConfigs reverses configs (:1862) so project (passed first) overrides global.

items.title: Given a global config roles.review.mode: 'quick' and a project .pan.yaml roles.review.mode: 'full', the merged config resolves roles.review.mode to 'full' (project overrides global)
Given neither global nor project sets roles.review.mode, the merged config defaults roles.review.mode to 'quick'
Given a config with roles.review.mode set to a value other than 'quick' or 'full', config load rejects it with a field-specific error from validateRoleFields
Add per-issue reviewMode field and the single resolveReviewMode helper behind isExtendedReviewEnabled(issueId?)

narrative.Action: Add `reviewMode?: 'quick' | 'full'` to the PanIssueRecord interface (src/lib/pan-dir/record.ts:99-122). Add ONE synchronous resolver resolveReviewMode(issueId?: string): 'quick' | 'full' that resolves per-issue (readIssueRecordSync(...).reviewMode) -> project/global (loadConfigSync().config.roles.review.mode) -> 'quick'. Change isExtendedReviewEnabled() (review-agent.ts:885) to isExtendedReviewEnabled(issueId?: string): boolean returning resolveReviewMode(issueId) === 'full'. The helper must stay synchronous and is the single source of mode truth; the per-issue read goes through the readIssueRecordSync door only. This bead is the foundation the spawn branch, dashboard gate, and CLI setter build on, so the contract (signature, resolution order, sync-only, single-helper) is what the inspector verifies.

items.title: Given an issue with no per-issue reviewMode and no config override, resolveReviewMode returns 'quick' and isExtendedReviewEnabled(issueId) returns false

Given a per-issue record with reviewMode 'full' while project and global resolve to 'quick', resolveReviewMode returns 'full' and isExtendedReviewEnabled(issueId) returns true (per-issue beats project and global)
Given global config roles.review.mode 'full' and no per-issue override, isExtendedReviewEnabled(issueId) returns true
isExtendedReviewEnabled returns its boolean synchronously and resolveReviewMode reads config and the per-issue record through loadConfigSync and readIssueRecordSync with no async I/O
Un-park the convoy fan-out behind isExtendedReviewEnabled in spawnReviewRoleForIssuePromise

narrative.Action: In spawnReviewRoleForIssuePromise (review-agent.ts ~:541-679) branch on isExtendedReviewEnabled(opts.issueId). full -> build buildReviewRolePrompt (synthesis prompt, :148) for the parent and un-comment + run the REVIEW_SUB_ROLES fan-out (the parked block at :644-674) via spawnReviewSubRoleForIssue, keeping it resume-aware (the sub-reviewer resume at :335-366 stays) and provider-default (pass through opts.model/opts.harness only — NO per-reviewer pickers). quick -> buildSelfReviewPrompt unchanged. The parent resume-vs-fresh logic (:591-617) is shared by both modes and must not regress. Run npm run typecheck after un-parking to catch any symbol drift in the parked block.

items.title: Given a full-mode issue, spawning review creates the parent agent-<id>-review (synthesis) plus four agent-<id>-review-<subRole> sessions for security/correctness/performance/requirements
Given a quick-mode issue, spawning review creates only the single agent-<id>-review self-review session and no sub-reviewer sessions
Given a full-mode issue that already reviewed once, a re-review RESUMES the existing sub-reviewer sessions (the count of sub-reviewer sessions/rollouts does not grow)
The spawn path passes only the issue's resolved model/harness to each convoy sub-reviewer — no per-reviewer harness or model picker is added
Pass issueId to the dashboard reviewer-tree gate so convoy lanes appear for full-mode issues

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/server/routes/reviewer-tree.ts:322 change `if (!isExtendedReviewEnabled()) return [];` to `if (!isExtendedReviewEnabled(opts.issueId)) return [];` (opts.issueId is already in scope, used at :333). This makes the dashboard surface the four convoy lanes for full-mode issues and none for quick-mode, sharing the single resolver with the spawn side.

items.title: Given a full-mode issue with active convoy sessions, buildReviewerNodes returns four reviewer lane nodes
Given a quick-mode issue, buildReviewerNodes returns an empty array (no phantom convoy lanes)
Add `pan review mode <id> <quick|full>` CLI subcommand and update the /pan-review skill

narrative.Action: Add src/cli/commands/review-mode.ts exporting reviewModeCommand(id, mode) that validates mode in {quick, full} and writes reviewMode onto the per-issue record via writeIssueRecordSync (using readIssueRecordSync/ensureIssueRecordSync to load-or-create first). Register it in the review block of src/cli/index.ts (~:355) as `review.command('mode <id> <mode>')`. Update skills/pan-review/SKILL.md to document the new subcommand in the SAME commit so scripts/lint-skills.sh (npm run lint) passes.

items.title: Running `pan review mode <id> full` persists reviewMode='full' on .pan/records/<id-lowercase>.json via the record write door
After `pan review mode <id> full`, resolveReviewMode(id) returns 'full' and isExtendedReviewEnabled(id) returns true
Running `pan review mode <id> bogus` rejects with a usage error and does not write the record
npm run lint passes — /pan-review SKILL.md documents the `mode` subcommand so scripts/lint-skills.sh finds no drift
Verify and test that convoy re-review resume routes through the correct per-harness transport

narrative.Action: Confirm the convoy sub-reviewer resume path (review-agent.ts:348-366) delivers through resumeAgent, which already routes per harness (agents.ts: pi->writePiAgentPrompt :4951, codex->deliverInitialPromptWithRetry :4962, claude-code->deliverResumeMessageWithTranscriptConfirmation :4984). Add a regression test asserting that a full-mode convoy re-review of a non-claude-code harness issue dispatches its sub-reviewer resume through that harness's transport (mock resumeAgent / delivery and assert the harness-specific branch is taken). No new transport code is added.

items.title: For a full-mode convoy re-review on a pi (non-claude-code) harness issue, a test asserts resumeAgent routes the sub-reviewer resume through the pi FIFO transport, not the claude-code path
For a claude-code full-mode convoy re-review, a test asserts resumeAgent routes the sub-reviewer resume through the supervisor/tmux delivery path
Re-verify the six parked convoy bugs against the resume-aware + reactive lifecycle

narrative.Action: Re-verify #1861, #1864, #1130, #1207, #838, and #1131 against the current convoy code (resume-aware sub-reviewers, reactive review.approved/test.passed handoffs, host-owned review-status write). For each, read the bug, check the relevant current code, and write a disposition: obsolete (with the code reason it no longer reproduces) or still-live. Fix any still-live bug that is in-scope for convoy revival; file a follow-on issue for anything out-of-scope. Do NOT blindly reactivate the bugs. Capture the per-bug dispositions in continue.json or a comment on PAN-1982.

items.title: Re-verification produces a written disposition (obsolete-with-code-reason or still-live) for each of #1861, #1864, #1130, #1207, #838, #1131
Every still-live in-scope bug is fixed and covered by a test that passes; every out-of-scope still-live bug produces a linked follow-on issue
